title: Consonants: Lip Sounds (पवर्ग)
description: Time for the labial consonants — sounds made using both lips! These are among the easiest Hindi sounds for English speakers since similar sounds exist in English. The five letters are प, फ, ब, भ, and म.
keyPoints: प (pa), फ (pha), ब (ba), भ (bha), म (ma) — the labial group
Both lips come together to produce these sounds
फ (pha) is NOT the English 'f' — it's an aspirated 'p' (lips together, puff of air)
म (ma) is the nasal sound of this group, made through the nose with lips closed

vietnamese: pha (p có bật hơi, KHÔNG phải 'f')

hint: Draw a vertical line on the right, then a horizontal line extending left at the top, with a small curve descending from it. It looks somewhat like a reversed 'P'.

memoryTrick: 'Machhli' — think 'much-lee' (the fish swims much and far!)

hint: Similar to ब but with an extra vertical stroke. Draw the main body of ब first, then add the additional stroke to make it भ.

title: Lip Sounds — Recap
takeaways: You learned 5 labial consonants: प (pa), फ (pha), ब (ba), भ (bha), म (ma)
These sounds are made with both lips — the most natural group for English speakers
Remember: फ (pha) is an aspirated 'p', NOT the English 'f' sound
New words: पंखा (fan), फूल (flower), बच्चा (child), भालू (bear), मछली (fish)
